macro_segments.title: Sanctifying Only the Skin of an Animal and Its Permissibility for Work

micro_segments.title: Abaye's Question to Rabba: Can You Work an Animal When Only Its Skin Is Sanctified?
Proof from the Fetus Case: Working Weakens the Fetus, Shearing Does Not
Rav Yosef's Response: Whether the Prohibition Is Biblical or Rabbinic
Discussion of Whether Working Actually Affects the Skin and Interpretation of the Sources

macro_segments.title: Mother Sanctified as Shelamim with Unsanctified Fetus: Shechting in the Temple and Issues of Act vs. Result

micro_segments.title: Abaye's Question: Mother Is Shelamim, Fetus Is Chullin, Shechted in the Temple—Is It Chullin Ba-azarah?
The Conceptual Issue: Was the Act or Result of Shechitah Performed on the Chullin?
Rav Yosef's Answer: The Pasuk Permits Shechitah Where Required, Not Where Alternatives Exist
Reversed Case: Mother Is Chullin, Fetus Is Shelamim, Shechted Outside—Is It Shechitas Kodashim Ba-chutz?
Rav Yosef's Second Answer: Only What Is Fit for the Mishkan Triggers the Prohibition

macro_segments.title: Mixtures and Derivatives: General Principle That Secondary Statuses Do Not Create Further Secondary Statuses

micro_segments.title: Overview: From Specific Terumah/Temurah Cases to a Series of Parallel Halakhic Structures
Meduma (Terumah Mixed with Chullin): Proportional Calculation, Not Treating Mixture as All Terumah
Mechometz (Yeast Causing Fermentation): Only Proportional Effect, Preventing Bitul
Mayim She'uvim (Drawn Water): Invalidating a Mikvah Only According to Proportion and Circumstances
Mei Chatat: The Mixture Cannot Make Other Water into Mei Chatat without Ashes
Beit Ha-peras: A Plowed-Over Field Cannot Make Another Field into Beit Ha-peras
Terumah: Terumah Cannot Be Separated Twice to Create New Terumah
Temurah: A Temurah Cannot Create Another Temurah, and a Newborn Cannot Create Temurah

macro_segments.title: Meduma (Mixed Terumah and Chullin): The Debate Between Rabbi Eliezer and the Sages on Proportional Treatment

micro_segments.title: Source of the Mishnah: The Position of the Sages (Not Rabbi Eliezer) That Mixtures Are Proportional
Rabbi Eliezer's Stricter Position: Treating the Meduma Entirely as Terumah
The Sages' Response: Proportional Effect Only According to Actual Ratio

macro_segments.title: Mechometz: Yeast Mixture and Fermentation—When Small Amounts Prevent Bitul

micro_segments.title: Case: Mixed Yeast (Chullin and Terumah) Falling into Dough—Does It Create Chametz?
Rabbi Eliezer: The Last (or Sequential) Yeast Determines if Fermentation Occurs
The Sages: Both Together (Zeh V'zeh Gorem) Cannot Prevent Bitul; Each Must Act Independently
Meaning of Lefi Cheshbon by Mechometz: Only if Terumah Yeast Alone Could Ferment Does It Matter

macro_segments.title: Mayim She'uvim: Drawn Water Invalidating a Mikvah—The Role of Amount, Vessels, and Natural Flow

micro_segments.title: Basic Rule: Three Log of Drawn Water Invalidates a Mikvah Before It Reaches Forty Se'ah
Rabbi Eliezer ben Yaakov: The Majority of Kosher Water Plus Running Drawn Water on Ground Permits the Mikvah
The Opposing View (Implied): All Drawn Water Invalidates Regardless of Majority Status
Rava's Interpretation: Lefi Cheshbon Refers to the Number of Vessels, Not Quantity Alone
Yosef ben Choni: Two or Three Vessels Invalidate, But Four or Five Do Not (Proportion of Acts)
Practical Application: Cleaning a Mikvah and Residual Water from Multiple Removals

macro_segments.title: Mei Chatat: The Recipe Requirement—Why a Mixture Cannot Serve as a Source for New Mei Chatat

micro_segments.title: The Mishnah's Simple Position: Mei Chatat Cannot Make Mei Chatat; It Requires Ashes and Water
Gemara's Attribution: This is the Position of the Sages, Not Rabbi Shimon
Rabbi Shimon's Lenient View: Either Ashes First or Water First Is Acceptable
Textual Proof: Analyzing Two Verses to Determine the Proper Order of Mixture
The Sages' Position: Water Must Come First; Ashes Added to Vessel, Not Vessel to Ashes

macro_segments.title: Beit Ha-peras: One Plowed-Over Field Cannot Create Another—The Debate Over Daisy-Chaining Impurity

micro_segments.title: The Mishnah's Position: A Beit Ha-peras Does Not Make Another Field into Beit Ha-peras
Rabbi Eliezer's Opposite View: One Beit Ha-peras Can Create Another Through Continued Plowing
Defining Beit Ha-peras: A Hundred Amah Radius of Spread When Plowing Over a Grave
Tosafot's Interpretation: The Disagreement Is About the Third Field, Not the Second; Derivative Status

Shechitas Kodashim ba-chutz (slaughtering sanctified animals outside the Temple)

Rabbi Eliezer ben Yaakov (position on drawn water and Mikvah)
Mei Chatat (water of purification from Red Heifer)
Rabbi Shimon (lenient position on Mei Chatat order)
Beit Ha-peras (field with scattered grave remains)
Yosef ben Choni (position on drawn water in multiple vessels)
